plan_name: Dual-listener serve infrastructure: TCP + Unix domain socket with graceful shutdown

tasks.name: Task 1: Dual-listener serve.rs with graceful shutdown
tasks.notes: Created crates/server/src/serve.rs with run_server function handling dual TCP+UDS listener startup and CancellationToken-based graceful shutdown. The async move wrapper pattern was needed for with_graceful_shutdown because CancellationToken::cancelled() borrows the token but spawned tasks require 'static lifetime. A supplementary commit (d22dfe5) added the mod serve declaration that was missed in the initial staging.

tasks.name: Task 2: CLI Serve command variant and main.rs integration
tasks.notes: Added Serve variant to Commands enum with --port (default 7424) and --socket (runtime-resolved via three-tier fallback). run_serve opens DB, builds SharedState with Arc::new(AppState), resolves socket path, and calls serve::run_server. The serve --help output correctly shows both options. Previously 27 dead-code warnings from API handlers are now resolved since build_router is reachable through the serve path.

decisions.decision: CancellationToken::cancelled() requires async move wrapper for 'static lifetime in tokio::spawn tasks
decisions.rationale: with_graceful_shutdown(tcp_token.cancelled()) fails because cancelled() borrows the token, but the spawned async block must be 'static. Wrapping in async move { token.cancelled().await } moves ownership of the token clone into the future, satisfying the lifetime requirement.
